title: Autonomous Drone Navigation in GPS-Denied Environments Using Neuromorphic Vision Sensors
abstract: This proposal develops a real-time autonomous navigation system for UAVs operating in GPS-denied indoor and subterranean environments using event-based neuromorphic cameras and spiking neural networks. Unlike conventional frame-based vision, neuromorphic sensors capture asynchronous pixel-level changes with microsecond latency and near-zero motion blur — enabling robust navigation in smoke-filled, low-light, or electromagnetically contested environments. Applications include disaster response, mine inspection, and defence. We have demonstrated proof-of-concept navigation in a controlled 200m corridor. We request funding for hardware scaling, algorithm development, and field trials in three target environments.
methodology_full: Phase 1 (Months 1-6): Integration of Prophesee Metavision EVK4 event camera with DJI Matrice 300 RTK platform. Development of event-stream preprocessing pipeline for spiking neural network input. Phase 2 (Months 7-14): Training of spiking neural network navigation policy using surrogate gradient methods. Sim-to-real transfer using CARLA-adapted event camera simulation. Phase 3 (Months 15-20): Field trials across three environments — collapsed building simulation (IIT Bombay structural lab), active mine environment (CMPDI Dhanbad), indoor industrial facility (partner site TBD). Phase 4 (Months 21-24): Performance benchmarking, safety analysis, publication, and IP filing.
requested_amount_detail: Budget covers hardware, personnel, field trial access fees, and IP filing costs across 24-month project duration.
team_full: Dr. Vikram Rao — PI, Associate Professor, Aerospace Engineering, IIT Bombay. Autonomous systems specialist. Director and 40% equity holder, NeuralDrone Pvt. Ltd.
Dr. Sunita Joshi — Co-PI, Electrical Engineering, IIT Bombay. Neuromorphic computing specialist.
Mr. Aditya Khanna — Research Assistant, M.Tech Robotics. Event camera integration.
Ms. Preethi Nair — Research Assistant, M.Tech Computer Science. Spiking neural network training.
references_full: Gallego G. et al. (2022). Event-based Vision: A Survey. IEEE TPAMI.
Mahowald M. (1992). VLSI Analogs of Neuronal Visual Processing. Caltech PhD Thesis.
Davies M. et al. (2018). Loihi: A Neuromorphic Manycore Processor. IEEE Micro.
Pfeiffer M. & Pfeil T. (2018). Deep Learning With Spiking Neurons. Frontiers in Neuroscience.
hidden_flaws: conflict_of_interest: PI Dr. Vikram Rao is a 40% equity holder and Director of NeuralDrone Pvt. Ltd. The budget includes a 24,000 USD technology transfer fee payable to NeuralDrone Pvt. Ltd. This creates a direct financial conflict of interest — public research funds would flow to a private company in which the PI holds significant equity. This is only detectable by cross-referencing the budget line item with the PI's affiliation in team composition.

clarification_map.neural drone: NeuralDrone Pvt. Ltd. is a technology commercialization vehicle associated with our research group. The technology transfer fee covers licensing of our preliminary event-camera preprocessing IP to the project.
clarification_map.conflict: Dr. Rao's involvement with NeuralDrone is disclosed in the team section as required by institutional policy. The arrangement has been reviewed by the institute's technology transfer office.
clarification_map.equity: Dr. Rao's equity position in NeuralDrone is a standard outcome of institute-supported commercialization activity and is governed by IIT Bombay's IP policy.
clarification_map.technology transfer: The technology transfer fee of $24,000 represents fair market value for licensing the preprocessing pipeline developed under prior internal funding.
clarification_map.budget: All budget line items are justified against DST procurement guidelines. Hardware costs reflect current market pricing for research-grade neuromorphic equipment.
clarification_map.NeuralDrone: NeuralDrone Pvt. Ltd. is registered under the Startup India scheme and operates independently from the research group.
clarification_map.interest: All potential conflicts of interest have been disclosed in accordance with DST guidelines. The institute's research integrity office has reviewed the arrangement.

default_clarification: The proposal has undergone internal review and all aspects comply with DST funding guidelines. We are happy to provide additional documentation upon request.
